Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories Set Three And To Extend Discovery Period
Off Calendar Mar-30-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF ANNIKA RODRIGUEZ's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories Set Three And To Extend Discovery Period.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's interrogatories are permissible only to identify relevant and admissible documents in defendant's possession and "the witnesses employed by or related to the defendant[s] who would be most competent to testify to those facts", because Plaintiff's previous motion for discovery of defendants' financial condition under Civil Code sec. 3295 was denied.

Section 3295 is intended both to protect financial privacy and to enable preparation for trial. For the latter purpose, the phrase "employed by or related to" can be read as "regularly used, consulted or relied upon" or "consistently connected with". See Rawnsley v. Superior Court, 183 Cal.App.3d 86, 91 (1986)(defendant may be required to "provide a list of witnesses who may be competent to testify about defendant's financial condition").

Accordingly, plaintiff's motion is granted to the following extent and otherwise denied as follows:

Special Interrogatory No. 110 - Defendants shall, within 15 days of the date of order hereon, each serve further responses identifying all persons regularly used, consulted or relied upon or consistently connected with them, if any, who are most competent to testify to any profits gained by defendants by wrongful conduct shown by the evidence and/or the financial condition of the defendants;

Special Interrogatory No. 111-113 - No further responses are required because Section 3295 does not authorize financial discovery, except as stated therein, without a finding of substantial probability that plaintiff will prevail on a claim under Section 3294, which finding has not been made. Plaintiff's cases are not to the contrary, except in instances where financial status is an element in a pending cause of action, a circumstance which is not present here.

The time for completion of discovery is not extended except for compliance with this order. This order does not authorize discovery except as expressly set forth herein.

Sanctions are denied because the circumstances make the imposition thereof unjust.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at jameshfleming@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: James Fleming; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
